title: ceratotheca triloba
intro: Ceratotheca triloba, also known as the purple allamanda or trilobed ceratotheca, is a flowering plant species in the bignonia family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and roots of Ceratotheca triloba have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
